name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 05.01.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 05.01.2026.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 02, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 634.14 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 617.74 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 02, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 634.14 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 617.74 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  02.01.2026, 99%  of  area has been  harvested  for Kharif crops all  over India. Approximately,  65.50%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 98.18% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (100%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (100%); 98.08% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (99.20%)  & Bajra (98.87%) and 99% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  02.01.2026, 99%  of  area has been  harvested  for Kharif crops all  over India. Approximately,  65.50%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 98.18% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (100%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (100%); 98.08% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (99.20%)  & Bajra (98.87%) and 99% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal in J&K, East Rajasthan, Gujarat, South Karnataka, Rayalaseema, many parts of Tamil Ndu and many parts of NE India. TMIN is likely to be below normal in East India, Central India, many parts of NW India, many parts of Maharashtra, CAP, Telangana and Kerala.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal in J&K, East Rajasthan, Gujarat, South Karnataka, Rayalaseema, many parts of Tamil Ndu and many parts of NE India. TMIN is likely to be below normal in East India, Central India, many parts of NW India, many parts of Maharashtra, CAP, Telangana and Kerala.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- January 04 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  58%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  81%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  58%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  97%;  and South Peninsula: 18%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- January 04 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  58%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  81%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  58%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  97%;  and South Peninsula: 18%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Subtropical westerly Jet Stream with core winds of the order of 130 knots at 12.6 km above mean sea level prevail over northwest India.
texts.text:  Subtropical westerly Jet Stream with core winds of the order of 130 knots at 12.6 km above mean sea level prevail over northwest India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated light/moderate rainfall/snowfall very likely over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-GilgitBaltistan-Muzaffarabad during 5th -6th January; Himachal Pradesh on 5th & 6th January 2026, and Uttarakhand on 6th January 2026.
texts.text:  Isolated light/moderate rainfall/snowfall very likely over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-GilgitBaltistan-Muzaffarabad during 5th -6th January; Himachal Pradesh on 5th & 6th January 2026, and Uttarakhand on 6th January 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Ground frost very likely at isolated places over Uttarakhand on 5th & 6th January 2026.
texts.text:  Ground frost very likely at isolated places over Uttarakhand on 5th & 6th January 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Western  Himalayan  Region,  Kerala,  Tamil  Nadu, Lakshadweep and Nicobar islands.
texts.text:  Isolated  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Western  Himalayan  Region,  Kerala,  Tamil  Nadu, Lakshadweep and Nicobar islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense to Very dense fog conditions very likely to continue during night/morning hours over North and adjoining central India during next 7 days.
texts.text:  Dense to Very dense fog conditions very likely to continue during night/morning hours over North and adjoining central India during next 7 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold  wave  conditions  very  likely  in  isolated  pockets  of  Punjab,  Haryana,  Chandigarh during 5th -8th; over West Rajasthan during 5th -9th; over East Rajasthan during 5th -10th; over Jharkhand on 6th -7th January 2026.
texts.text:  Cold  wave  conditions  very  likely  in  isolated  pockets  of  Punjab,  Haryana,  Chandigarh during 5th -8th; over West Rajasthan during 5th -9th; over East Rajasthan during 5th -10th; over Jharkhand on 6th -7th January 2026.

texts.label: page_footer

texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 01 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 142.177 billion cubic meter, which is 77.4%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 105.58 percent of last year's storage and 122.53 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 01 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 142.177 billion cubic meter, which is 77.4%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 105.58 percent of last year's storage and 122.53 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (25 Dec - 31 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 95%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 31 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 11%.
texts.text:  During the week (25 Dec - 31 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 95%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 31 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 11%.

texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  below  over  the  country  except  Tamil  Nadu  and Kerala, where it is likely to be near normal to above normal.
texts.text:  Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  below  over  the  country  except  Tamil  Nadu  and Kerala, where it is likely to be near normal to above normal.

texts.orig:  Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of the country except northeast India  &  Western Himalayan Region and many parts of northwest India, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week.

texts.text: Week 2 (09 Jan - 15 Jan 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of the country except  some  parts  of  the  Western  Himalayan  Region,  where  these  are  likely  to  be  above normal by 1-3°C during the week.
texts.text:  Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of the country except  some  parts  of  the  Western  Himalayan  Region,  where  these  are  likely  to  be  above normal by 1-3°C during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold  wave  conditions  are  likely  over  some  parts  of  Punjab,  Haryana,  North  Rajasthan, North  Madhya  Pradesh,  interior  Odisha, adjoining parts of west Jharkhand, north Chhattisgarh, and south Uttar Pradesh during some days of the week.
texts.text:  Cold  wave  conditions  are  likely  over  some  parts  of  Punjab,  Haryana,  North  Rajasthan, North  Madhya  Pradesh,  interior  Odisha, adjoining parts of west Jharkhand, north Chhattisgarh, and south Uttar Pradesh during some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense fog conditions are likely to prevail during early morning hours in isolated pockets of north India on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Dense fog conditions are likely to prevail during early morning hours in isolated pockets of north India on some days of the week.

texts.text:  Rapeseed & Mustard : About 89.36 (113% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Rapeseed &  Mustard  has  been  reported  as  compared  to  86.57  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding period of last year.
